title: Queensland (QLD)
description:
6 j
Poultry farm work
Do you care about your work & want to do a good job?
Not afraid of physical work?
Are someone who is reliable ?
Consider work in our poultry farms with a friendly, multi-cultural team
No experience necessary, just physical fitness and a good work ethic
Flexible days of work available
is able to work weekends
Casual rates
Birthday Day off after probation period
About the Role
Darwalla is seeking applications for people who care about their job, to work alongside our experienced teams in our poultry farms, located between Warwick & Toowoomba, on the beautiful Southern Darling Downs.
The successful candidates will be required to carry out a wide range of physical tasks including walking in sheds, egg collecting, egg grading & packing and cleaning sheds. Full training is provided, so no experience is necessary, however a willingness to learn and work hard is essential.
Generally we work across 7 days/week. The number of hours per week may vary .Our farms are located in rural locations between Warwick & Toowoomba, plus out at Killarney & down at Laidley, so a driver's license and access to reliable transport is necessary.
The poultry industry has very strict bio-security and animal welfare standards, so all employees are restricted from keeping pet birds, poultry and pigs and residing with others who work at other poultry or pig farming work sites. Therefore, if you have poultry, birds or pigs kept at your house, I'm sorry but you cannot work for Darwalla. Our farms are all shower-on & shower-off facilities to protect our birds from disease. Our showers are hot and we supply towels, uniforms and all safety equipment.
Skills & Experience
Successful candidates must have the following attributes:
Meet bio-security requirements
Physically fit & hard working
Reliable & self-motivated
Team player
Have a mature & responsible attitude
Good communicator
Able to follow instructions
Car and Licence
Have a tolerance to working in a poultry environment, which can be dusty, noisy and smelly
good understanding of English, both written and oral
About Darwalla
Darwalla has a diverse workforce and welcomes applicants who are as passionate as we are about producing quality chickens. As a fourth-generation family business, Darwalla started producing chickens over 90 years in Mt Cotton, with farms now located in the Redlands, on the Darling Downs and in the Lockyer Valley.
Safety is a priority of ours and we look after our people. You will be provided with a uniform, training & relevant safety equipment.
If you meet the above criteria and you’re interested in this role, we want to hear from you!
APPLY NOW!!
https://darwalla.expr3ss.com/jobDetails?selectJob=95&s=6

title: Queensland (QLD)
description:

Line Crew Labourers - QLD
The Role
Join a dynamic and hardworking team supporting Line Crew Labourers across Queensland. This entry-level, labour-intensive role involves working outdoors in remote areas, contributing to essential fieldwork that requires accuracy, physical fitness, and a safety-first mindset. You'll be part of a team that values communication, teamwork, and adaptability in constantly changing field environments.
Key Responsibilities
This role supports the full lifecycle of nodal equipment deployment, collection and preparation to ensure projects run efficiently and safely.
Layout, pickup, and storage of nodal equipment
Placing nodes in the ground using a small mattock and initialising
Using GPS tablets to identify precise placement locations
Manual handling of nodes weighing 1-2kg (crates up to 10kg)
Collection of nodes from placement locations
Dismantling nodes for battery storage/charging and data module preparation
Traversing changing and difficult terrain on foot or in company 4WDs
Working up to 12-hour days (11 working hours + 1-hour lunch break)
Benefits
$364.63 per day
Variable duration depending on site and project needs
Up to a 12-hour work day with variable rosters from 6/1 through to 28/14
Work in a diverse range of outdoor and remote settings
Gain experience in resource-sector field operations
All equipment and training provided
Your Skills & Experience
Australian Manual Driver's Licence
Physically able to undertake labour-intensive work in hot and remote environments
Ability to lift 25kg repetitively
Flexible and adaptable to changing rosters and project needs
Strong awareness of safety procedures in challenging environments
Job Application Form

title: Queensland (QLD)
description:
28 mars
Strawberry Runner - Armidale NSW and Ballandean QLD
The Role
We're seeking Strawberry Runners to join a busy horticultural production team at a leading nursery in Ballandean, Queensland and Armidale NSW. This is a hands-on role in the agriculture and plant production industry, involving physical and detailed work in an indoor nursery environment. You'll be part of a friendly, hardworking crew focused on producing high-quality strawberry runners for dispatch across Australia.
Key Responsibilities
In this role, you'll play a key part in supporting efficient nursery operations and maintaining product quality. Your day-to-day duties will include:
Handling and processing plug plants within the production shed, including grading, trimming, cleaning, and packing.
Inspecting plants for quality, uniformity, and signs of damage or disease.
Preparing trays and packaging materials for daily dispatch.
Following hygiene, biosecurity, and safety protocols at all times.
Assisting with basic machinery such as conveyors or trimming tools.
Keeping work areas clean and organised.
Recording output data and reporting issues to supervisors.
Supporting general shed and nursery operations, including palletising and labelling.
Benefits
Pay Rate: $31.19 per hour
Duration: 3 weeks
Hours: 38 hours per week

About the Business
We are a leading macadamia grower based in Bundaberg, supplying premium nut-in-shell products to export markets. Our orchard is part of a growing agribusiness committed to sustainable farming practices and innovation in horticulture.

The Orchard Farm Hand plays a key role in supporting the daily operations of our macadamia orchard. This hands-on position involves a variety of tasks across both harvest and non-harvest periods, contributing to the health and productivity of the orchard.

Perform general orchard maintenance including spraying, weeding, slashing, pruning, mulching, and cleaning

title: Queensland (QLD)
description:
19 mars
Cleaner / Gardener - Outdoor & Indoor Variety
Love variety in your work? Join our award-winning team at Blue Property Services, a leading strata and commercial property maintenance company in Brisbane. With multiple industry awards and a strong reputation for reliability, we take pride in keeping properties looking their best.
As part of a 2-person team, you’ll enjoy a mix of indoor and outdoor tasks maintaining gardens, offices, and commercial buildings across Brisbane — with hands-on training to build your skills and grow with us.
THE ROLE
Full-time, 7:00 AM – 3:30 PM (Mon–Fri), with overtime as needed. You’ll handle cleaning, gardening and grounds maintenance at apartment complexes, business offices, and retail/office building common areas ensuring sites are clean and well-presented.
KEY DUTIES
Cleaning & Presentation
Cleaning and upkeep of strata and commercial common areas.
Office and retail building bathroom/amenities cleaning and restocking supplies.
Sweeping, vacuuming, mopping, dusting, and cobweb removal.
Spot-cleaning external areas such as glass doors, handrails, and signage.
Outdoor cleaning (paths, driveways, entryways, building surrounds).
Bin management (placing bins out/in, bin washing, keeping bin areas tidy).
Gardening & Grounds
Lawn mowing, hedge trimming, pruning, and general plant care.
Weed control (including hand weeding and safe use of herbicides).
Mulching, planting, watering, and garden refurbishments.
Tree and shrub maintenance (small-scale).
Operation of mowers, trimmers, blowers, and other tools.
Green-waste removal and transport to recovery centres.
Other Responsibilities
Operate job management app for scheduling, reporting, and uploading site photos.
Work safely in line with WHS procedures and report hazards promptly.
Communicate effectively with management, teammates, and clients.
Proactively identify issues and assist in problem-solving.
Keep company vehicles and equipment clean.
WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR
Valid driver’s licence (essential).
Comfortable working in a 2-person team.
Experience in cleaning/gardening (preferred — training provided).
Queensland White Card (or willing to obtain).
Strong attention to detail.
Well-presented, reliable, and able to perform routine tasks without supervision.
Good organisation and time management.
Great physical condition — able to carry out physically demanding tasks and work safely in all weather conditions (heat, cold, rain).
Bonus: First Aid, Horticulture, or Landscaping qualifications.
Benefits
Pay based on the Cleaning Services Award.
Stable full-time role with consistent hours.
Award-winning company and supportive culture.
Variety of indoor & outdoor work.
Uniform, tools and equipment provided.
Ongoing training.
Working Holiday Visa holders are welcome.
HOW TO APPLY
Email your resume (and a brief note about your experience and availability) to job@blueservices.com.au with the subject: “Cleaner/Gardener Application – [Your Name]”

The Barmen are an exclusive bartending service that cater to a variety of events, from Birthdays, to Weddings and Hen’s Parties. We are a high-class, professional service that delivers an exceptional level of service to our clients.
We are looking to add to our roster staff who would be available for majorly weekend work at our events. We are creating a network of staff that we can contact when we require staff to assist with our events.

Extensive bartending or cocktail experience is ideal but not necessary, as all training will be provided. Great opportunity for current Hospo staff to change it up, or for full time staff to earn a bit of extra coin on the side.

Hatchery Assistant
We've been proudly bringing joy to tables and smiles to faces with the delicious food we've been crafting for over 100 years.
A large part of our strong growth and success is because we're always looking for new ways to do things and thinking about how we can create a better, more sustainable future world together.
Our success as an industry leader in providing deliciously good food means we can continue to invest in our future as we work towards becoming Australia and New Zealand's first choice for poultry.
Your opportunity to join our team:
We are currently looking for a Hatchery Assistant to join our team at our McKees Hill Hatchery. This is a hands-on role working in a fast-paced production environment, supporting the day-to-day operations of our hatchery and helping ensure the highest standards of animal welfare, quality, and safety are maintained.
This role will work 3-4 days per week, including Saturdays, with 6:00 am starts, so reliability and the ability to commit to early morning shifts are essential.
Benefits
Immediate start
Onsite Parking
Great employee benefits including product discounts, travel discounts + more!
Great job security with an ASX-listed and stable company
The health, safety, and well-being of our people is our highest priority
Fast-paced and team-focused environment
Consistent hours provided weekly
Daily Tasks:
Ensure all livestock is treated humanely and in line with Inghams’ Minimum Standard Procedures
Process and inspect eggs and chicken safely and efficiently in accordance with quality standards
Complete manual handling tasks safely and correctly
Be an active, respectful team member who is reliable and committed to their work
Actively support a strong safety culture by following procedures, speaking up, and being mindful of risks
Perform cleaning duties and maintain a clean and organised work environment
Store and maintain materials, equipment, and tools appropriately
Identify and escalate any Animal Welfare, Food Safety & Quality issues, hazards, or risks
Take personal responsibility to ensure the humane treatment of livestock at all times
You will bring to this role:
The ability to perform repetitive tasks accurately and consistently in line with policies and standards
Strong attention to detail and the ability to follow instructions and procedures correctly
Proven experience working effectively in a team environment
Clear communication skills, both written and verbal
A strong work ethic, reliability, and motivation to do your best each day
The ability to remain calm, focused, and solutions-oriented when challenges arise
We're looking for people who are curious, caring, courageous, and committed to join us. People who want to contribute their best work every single day and continue delivering delicious food that's... Always Good.

title: Queensland (QLD)
description:
14 mars
Livestock Farmhand | Piggery Attendant
Position Details
Title: Piggery Attendant
Location: Millmerran, Queensland
Hours: Monday to Friday - 38hrs per week
Roster: Working every 2nd weekend (6 hours Saturday + 6 hours Sunday)
Reporting to: Farm Manager
SunPork Company Benefits
At SunPork we offer stable employment with a large Australian agribusiness where safety, teamwork and respect are valued.
You'll enjoy:
Stable employment within a structured farming environment
Supportive team with on-the-job training
Opportunity to develop livestock production skills
Being part of a trusted Australian farming business
Employee discounts across 400+ retailers
Opportunity
We are seeking a Piggery Attendant to join our farming operations in Millmerran, Queensland.
This hands-on role supports the daily care and management of pigs across the farm, helping to ensure high standards of animal welfare, biosecurity and farm productivity. The role is suited to someone with some experience working with livestock or within farming environments, or someone looking to further develop their skills in livestock production.
Working as a part of a small farm team, you'll assist with feeding, monitoring pig health, maintaining clean facilities and supporting breeding and production activities across the site.
Key Responsibilities
Feed pigs and monitor feed and water intake
Observe livestock and identify signs of illness or injury
Assist with treatments, vaccinations and health monitoring
Maintain clean pig sheds, pens and equipment
Follow strict biosecurity, hygiene and animal welfare procedures
About You
You're reliable, practical and comfortable working in a hands-on farming environment. You may already have some experience with livestock or farming, or be looking to build your skills in agriculture.
You will bring:
Some livestock or farming experience (desirable but not essential)
Strong work ethic and reliability
Ability to work both independently and as part of a team
Physical fitness for hands-on farm work
Commitment to animal welfare and biosecurity standards
Current drivers licence
Who We Are
SunPork Group is Australia's leading farmer-owned and operated pork producer, delivering premium Australian pork from farm to plate. With operations across Australia and New Zealand, our farming teams play a critical role in maintaining strong animal welfare standards, efficient production systems and safe farming environments.
Working within SunPork's farming operations means being part of a team committed to responsible livestock care, safety and continuous improvement across our farms.
SunPork Group is an equal opportunity employer. Applicants must have the right to work in Australia. Successful candidates will be asked to undergo a pre-employment medical assessment which will include a drug and alcohol test.
Ready to join the team?
If you enjoy working with animals or want to build a long-term career in agriculture, we'd love to hear from you.
Apply now
https://sunporkgroup.elmotalent.com.au/careers/default/job/view/133

title: Queensland (QLD)
description:
12 mars
Stockhandler/Site Maintenance Officer & Administration Officer
Emerald Saleyards, QLD
✅ Apply as a couple or individually
✅ Work with cattle, learn new skills, and grow your career
✅ Flexible options (full-time, part-time or casual depending on the role)
We are looking for two people to join the Outcross Agri-Services team at Emerald Saleyards. These roles could suit a couple or individual applicants and offer flexible arrangements depending on experience and availability.
About Outcross Agri-Services
Outcross Agri-Services is one of Australia’s leading livestock contracting companies, working in major saleyards across the country. Each year the team manages the sale of over 1.1 million cattle and 4.2 million sheep, with operations across QLD, NSW, VIC and NT. The Emerald Saleyards contract is an important part of their Queensland operations.
Role - Stockhandler/Site Maintenance Officer
This is a hands-on role working with cattle and assisting with the day-to-day running of the saleyards. You will work closely with the Saleyard Manager, handling livestock safely and keeping the yards maintained to a high standard. This role offers a genuine pathway to progress into a Saleyard Manager position in the future.
Skills & experience we’re looking for:
• Experience with livestock handling (cattle preferred)
• Practical maintenance and machinery skills (Skid Steer experience is a plus)
• Strong work ethic, reliability, and the ability to work safely around animals
• Leadership potential and willingness to learn management skills
Role - Administration Officer
This role involves supporting sale day operations and keeping the office running efficiently.
This role can be full-time or part-time, however Thursday and Friday are essential days.
Skills & experience we’re looking for:
• Strong administration, organisational and communication skills
• Good attention to detail
• Confidence learning new software
• Livestock or saleyard experience (highly regarded but not essential)
Accommodation
A studio apartment in Emerald is available, which may suit a couple or a single person relocating for the role. Outcross may also assist in sourcing longer-term accommodation locally.
If you’re ready to work hands-on with livestock, take initiative, and grow your career in a practical, fast-paced environment, we’d love to hear from you. APPLY NOW. Please email your cover letter and resume to jobs@annabrown.com.au or call for more information. Contact either Anna on 0427 644 527 or Kate on 0428 731 447.

At Rombola Family Farms, we’re not just about farming – we’re about crafting a sustainable future. title: Queensland (QLD)
description:
9 mars
Casual Field Hands - Burdekin
Sugar Research Australia (SRA) are looking for Casual Field Hands for up to 5 days per week for work from April through to November 2026 (depending on operational needs).
The role
Activities will include:
Planting sugarcane, frequently cutting cane using a cane knife, trimming, bundling, carrying and loading sugarcane stalks in the field.
Collecting samples including soil, stalk, and leaf.
Working, standing, and walking long distances in the field for long periods on uneven ground in cane fields.
Frequently bending, twisting, squatting, often under weight.
Any other specific duties and instructions that will be provided by your supervisor.
Who are you?
It is essential that you:
Are able to perform physical, manual labour in very hot and humid conditions.
Are physically fit and can lift bundles of cane repeatedly over several hours (up to 20kg).
Are happy to pay attention to detail and can follow instructions and carry out work safely and accurately.
Are punctual and reliable.
Are committed to safe work practices.
Have reliable transport to and from site and a current driver’s licence.
If this sounds like you, and you want to work as part of a great supportive team playing a critical role in SRA operations, contact (07) 4783 8600 to submit your application, no later than 3 April 2026.
Applicants must have the right to work in Australia to apply. Successful applicants will also be required to undertake a medical assessment (functional and drug and alcohol screening) plus a VEVO check for visa holders.

title: Queensland (QLD)
description:
9 mars
Farm Hands - Adare Farm (Gatton, QLD 4343)
Farm Hands – Adare Farm (Gatton, QLD 4343)
• Are you a hard worker with experience in agriculture?
• Trying to find a casual opportunity near Gatton, QLD 4343?
• Looking to learn on the job? Undertake further study?
• Do you want to work for a company that is willing to invest in you?
Grow your career with ProTen….
About the role
We are looking for Farm Hands to join our teams in current vacancies on our Adare Farm (Gatton, QLD 4343). You will be working alongside managers with day-to-day work on the poultry farms. With the scope to progress into future management roles. Duties will include but not limited to:
• Working as part of an effective and efficient team, including following direction from farm manager and assistant farm manager
• Working with livestock daily and making sure bird health and wellbeing is excellent.
• Daily shed routine checks, record keeping, grounds maintenance
• Completing minor repairs, reporting repairs to be made.
• Making sure company policies and biosecurity measures are followed at all times.
About Us
ProTen is Australia's largest corporate meat chicken grower, setting the standard in broiler farm operations and development. With over 720 poultry sheds across 62+ broiler farms strategically located in New South Wales, Victoria, South Australia, Western Australia, and Queensland, ProTen is at the forefront of the industry. Our farms have the capacity to produce over 174 million broiler chickens annually, contributing approximately 25% of Australia's total broiler chicken production.
At ProTen, we are driven by a simple but powerful mission - "We grow the chickens”. Our role is much more than just producing food—it's about contributing to the health and well-being of every Australian. We are dedicated to helping our customers ensure that all Australians have access to an affordable, sustainable, healthy, and complete diet.
This mission is something we take to heart, and it's why we are committed to caring for our people, our communities, our animals, and the land we work on. It’s not just what we do, it’s who we are. Which is underpinned by our core values of “Teamwork, Professional, Communication, Caring and Curious”.
About You
To thrive in this role, you’ll need:
• Previous experience with Poultry Farming is not essential but would be highly regarded.
• A proactive, detail-oriented, and hands-on approach to farm management.
• High regard for Safety, Health, Environment, and Quality (SHEQ) standards, promoting a positive WHS culture.
• Physical fitness to perform farm duties, supported by a pre-employment functional assessment (including drug and alcohol testing).
• The right to work in Australia.
We’re looking for someone passionate, open-minded, and driven to lead a high-performing team in a dynamic environment.
Important Information: Due to strict quarantine requirements, we cannot accept applications from individuals who:
• Regularly come into contact with pigs or birds, including those kept at your residence.
• Live with someone who works in other poultry or pig operations.
How to Apply
• If this sounds like the opportunity you’ve been waiting for, APPLY NOW!
• For more information about ProTen and what we do, visit our website: www.ProTen.com.au
• Take the next step in your career and join an industry leader
For more information on Proten and how we can work with you,
please contact us via phone or email.
Email: headoffice@proten.com.au
Tel: +61 2 9458 1700

Activities will include:
Planting sugarcane, frequently cutting cane using a cane knife, trimming, bundling, carrying and loading sugarcane stalks in the field.

title: Queensland (QLD)
description:
27 fév.
General Farmhands
About Us
Sutton Farms is a family-owned and operated fresh produce business based in the Lockyer Valley. We specialize in growing high-quality tomatoes, spring onions, lettuce, wombok, and other small crops year-round, delivering fresh produce to our customers nearly every day.
With approximately 900 acres of cultivated land across multiple sites in the Lockyer Valley and Darling Downs, we supply all major central markets along the east coast and leading supermarket chains, making our produce available in most states of Australia.
Our diverse team of over 150 comes from various backgrounds and nationalities, all working together to bring fresh, locally grown produce to Australian tables.
As we look ahead to 2025, Sutton Farms is set to produce more than ever before. To meet this growing demand, we're expanding our team and looking for eager individuals to join us on this journey.
The Role
Sutton Farms is looking for General Farmhands to join our team in the Lockyer Valley. While relevant farm and crop experience will be looked upon very favourably, farm experience is not necessary. The successful applicant will require:
Willingness to learn and perform tasks to the required standards
Ability to follow direction from supervisors
Good communication skills
Own reliable transport and licence
All duties are general farm duties and include, but are not limited to:
Planting seedlings
Tomato work (trellising, pull wires, run wires, pull pegs)
Irrigation (lay trickle, set out pipes, lift trickle)
Tractor driving
Harvesting
If you have had previous industry experience with the tasks mentioned, please outline this in your application. Please send your cover letter and resume to Sutton Farms’ office email – jobs@suttonfarms.com.au
Successful applicants will be contacted for interview. Remuneration will depend on industry knowledge and experience, with growth and career progression based on performance and attitude.

description:

Section Waiter
About Anyday
At Anyday, we create places that feel good to be in—spaces shaped by genuine hospitality, a deep love for food and a belief in the quiet power of connection. Our venues are guided by instinct, not formulas, offering warmth, character, and moments that linger long after the table’s been cleared.
Founded in 2014, Anyday’s current portfolio includes Agnes, Idle, Biànca, hôntô, LOS, sAme sAme, Golden Avenue, and the newly opened The French Exit - with plans for many more.
About The French Exit
The French Exit is Brisbane’s ode to Paris, alive with clatter, candlelight, and the European perfume of butter and wine. Steak frites, duck à l’orange, escargot, and soufflé are served with soul, while wine makes for the heartbeat with over five hundred bottles carefully chosen. The tables are close, the energy unfiltered, and every detail whispers 'bistrot'.
The Role
The French Exit is seeking a passionate and professional Section Waiter to join our front-of-house team. In this role, you will be responsible for managing your section during service, guiding guests through the menu with warmth and confidence, anticipating their needs and ensuring every detail of the experience reflects the high standards we hold ourselves to.
Previous experience in the hospitality industry in a fast-paced environment
A genuine passion for delivering an exceptional dining experience
Confidence in managing a full section independently, especially during busy services
Strong communication and interpersonal skills
A proactive approach and ability to stay composed under pressure
A sharp eye for detail and a polished, professional manner
Commitment to supporting the team in creating a cohesive and uplifting workplace
RSA & Food Safety Certificate required.
What's In It For You
One extra week of annual leave based on length of service
Referral scheme of up to $1,000 for referring a friend
Restaurant discounts of up to 25% off
Team meals
Annual Team Party
Company wide sports teams and initiatives
A wide variety of discounts on a range of participating retailers
Learning and development opportunities, including WSET and leadership training
How To Apply

https://anyday-restaurant.applynow.net.au/jobs/ANY460-section-waiter

description:

Strawberry Runner - Armidale NSW and Ballandean QLD
The Role
We're seeking Strawberry Runners to join a busy horticultural production team at a leading nursery in Ballandean, Queensland and Armidale NSW. This is a hands-on role in the agriculture and plant production industry, involving physical and detailed work in an indoor nursery environment. You'll be part of a friendly, hardworking crew focused on producing high-quality strawberry runners for dispatch across Australia.
Key Responsibilities
In this role, you'll play a key part in supporting efficient nursery operations and maintaining product quality. Your day-to-day duties will include:
Handling and processing plug plants within the production shed, including grading, trimming, cleaning, and packing.
Inspecting plants for quality, uniformity, and signs of damage or disease.
Preparing trays and packaging materials for daily dispatch.
Following hygiene, biosecurity, and safety protocols at all times.
Assisting with basic machinery such as conveyors or trimming tools.
Keeping work areas clean and organised.
Recording output data and reporting issues to supervisors.
Supporting general shed and nursery operations, including palletising and labelling.
Benefits
Pay Rate: $31.19 per hour
Duration: 3 months (seasonal role)
Hours: 38 hours per week
Opportunities for overtime during peak production periods.
Gain valuable experience in the horticulture and nursery industry.
Be part of a welcoming, safety-first workplace culture.
Your Skills & Experience
Reliable, punctual, and able to work in a fast-paced environment.
Physically fit and comfortable performing repetitive tasks and standing for long periods.
Strong attention to detail and quality standards.
Positive team player who values communication and safe work practices.
Previous experience in nursery, horticultural, or food/produce packing environments. (Desirable)
Job Application Form

description:

Piggery Attendant (Farm Hand)
Who We Are
SunPork Group is Australia's leading farmer-owned and operated pork-producer, with farming operations across Australia and New Zealand. Our farming business unit is responsible for the day-to-day care, welfare and production of our livestock across a network of pig farms, operating to high standards of animal welfare, safety and biosecurity.
As a Piggery Attendant at SunPork, you'll be part of an established on-farm team focused on doing the basics well - caring for animals, maintaining clean and safe facilities, and supporting smooth farm operations.
Opportunity
We're seeking a reliable and motivated Piggery Attendant to join our farming team at our site in Thyra, NSW - approximately a 30 minute drive north of Echuca, VIC. This is a hands-on role suited to people who enjoy practical work, working with animals and being part of a team that takes pride in doing things well.
SunPork Company Benefits
At SunPork, we offer stable employment within a large, Australian-owned farming business where safety, animal welfare and teamwork come first. Our on-farm roles are supported, with the tools and structure needed to do the job well.
You'll enjoy:
Stable, ongoing employment
A supportive team environment with hands-on training
Opportunities to develop new skills and learn different areas of the farm
Being part of a trusted Australian farming business
Employee discounts across 400+ retailers
About the Role
Your key responsibilities will include:
Daily care and observation of livestock to support health and wellbeing
Feeding, cleaning and general husbandry tasks
Assisting with on-farm operations in line with production requirements
Maintain hygiene, biosecurity and animal welfare standards
Operating basic farm equipment and complete routine tasks
Working safely and following farm procedures at all times
About You
You don't need prior piggery experience - just the right attitude and willingness to learn.
You will bring:
A genuine interest in agriculture and working with animals
Reliability, strong work ethic and willingness to learn
Ability to work as part of a team and follow instructions
Comfort working in a physical, hands-on environment
Commitment to safety, animal welfare and biosecurity
SunPork Group is an equal opportunity employer. Applicants must have the right to work in Australia. As part of our recruitment process, candidates may be required to undertake a pre-employment medical assessment.
Ready to Join Us?
If you're looking for a practical, rewarding role and want to build on your experience in Australian agriculture, apply now and join the SunPork team.
Apply through our website:
https://sunporkgroup.elmotalent.com.au/careers/default/job/view/123

title: Queensland (QLD)
description:
17 fév.
Landscape Construction Crew Member
Location: Port Douglas, QLD
Company: Hortulus Landscapes, a leading landscape design & construction firm.
Are you a passionate and reliable individual with experience in landscape construction? We're seeking a dedicated Crew Member to join our dynamic team in the beautiful Port Douglas area. This is an excellent opportunity to work on exciting, high-quality design projects encompassing both stunning hardscapes and vibrant softscapes.
About Us
We are a reputable landscape design and construction company known for our innovative designs and exceptional craftsmanship. We value quality work, a strong team ethic, and professional growth. With a full pipeline of exciting projects ahead, we need an experienced and motivated individual to help us bring these designs to life.
The Role
As a Landscape Construction Crew Member, you will be involved in all aspects of project installation. This role is physically demanding and requires resilience in varying weather conditions typical of the Port Douglas climate (warm and rainy).
Key Responsibilities:
Hardscape Construction: Assisting with the building of structural elements such as retaining walls, paving, irrigation, rock work and basic concreting.
Softscape Installation: Preparing soil, planting trees, shrubs, and laying turf.
Site Preparation & Maintenance: General site cleanup, material handling, and equipment operation as required.
Team Collaboration: Working effectively and efficiently with the existing crew to meet project deadlines and maintain high standards of quality.
We Are Looking For:
Experience: Prior experience in the landscape construction industry is required.
Qualifications (Ideal): A formal qualification (e.g., Certificate III in Landscape Construction) is highly regarded but not essential if you can demonstrate solid practical experience.
Physical Fitness: The role requires physical strength and stamina to perform manual labour in warm and humid conditions.
Reliability: You must be punctual, dependable, and a committed team player.
Driver's Licence: A current, valid Australian driver's licence is essential.
Work Ethic: A positive attitude, willingness to learn, and commitment to delivering high-quality work.
What We Offer:
Exciting Projects: Opportunity to work on diverse, high-end landscape design projects.
Professional Development: For the ideal candidate, we offer further on-the-job training and support for formal TAFE studies (e.g., Certificate III in Landscape Construction) to help you advance your career.
Supportive Environment: Join a friendly, professional team that values hard work and collaboration.
Competitive Renumeration: Wages commensurate with experience and qualifications.
Ready to get your hands dirty and build beautiful spaces in Port Douglas?
If you have the experience, the right attitude, and are ready for a new challenge, please email your resume and a brief cover letter outlining your relevant experience to info@hortulus.com.au
Only shortlisted candidates with relevant experience will be contacted.

Grain Handler / Forklift Operator - Goondiwindi, QLD
The Role
We are seeking Grain Handlers to join our grain harvest in Goondiwindi. This role is within the grain storage and transport sector, supporting daily grain-handling operations at a busy regional site. You'll be working in an outdoor, physical environment that can involve heat and dust, as part of a practical, hard-working team. The role suits someone experienced with grain operations who can work safely, communicate clearly, and contribute during peak receival and load-out periods.
Key Responsibilities
The purpose of this role is to support efficient grain handling, storage, and dispatch while maintaining safety and quality standards. Your responsibilities will include:
Rolling and securing tarps on grain stacks
Moving grain and loading out trucks
Operating loaders and forklifts safely
Sampling grain and monitoring quality
Weighing trucks and completing basic records
Directing trucks on site to ensure safe traffic flow
Maintaining general site cleanliness and safety
Following all workplace health and safety procedures
Benefits
$33.38 per hour with consistent, ongoing work
Casual-style opportunity with ongoing duration
Reliable hours of approximately 40 - 60 hours per week
Hands-on experience operating loaders and forklifts
Work in a regional location with a strong focus on safety and teamwork
Your Skills & Experience
Previous experience working in grain handling or grain storage
Grain sampling experience
Forklift operating experience
Physically fit and able to work in heat and dusty conditions
Strong English communication skills for safety and coordination
Reliable, safety-conscious, and able to work as part of a team

About the Role
Darwalla is seeking applications for people who care about their job, to work alongside our experienced teams in our poultry farms, located between Warwick & Toowoomba, on the beautiful Southern Darling Downs.
The successful candidates will be required to carry out a wide range of physical tasks including walking in sheds, egg collecting, egg grading & packing and cleaning sheds. Full training is provided, so no experience is necessary, however a willingness to learn and work hard is essential.
Generally we work across 7 days/week. The number of hours per week may vary .Our farms are located in rural locations between Warwick & Toowoomba, plus out at Killarney & down at Laidley, so a driver's license and access to reliable transport is necessary.
The poultry industry has very strict bio-security and animal welfare standards, so all employees are restricted from keeping pet birds, poultry and pigs and residing with others who work at other poultry or pig farming work sites. Therefore, if you have poultry, birds or pigs kept at your house, I'm sorry but you cannot work for Darwalla. Our farms are all shower-on & shower-off facilities to protect our birds from disease. Our showers are hot and we supply towels, uniforms and all safety equipment.
Skills & Experience
Successful candidates must have the following attributes:
Meet bio-security requirements
Physically fit & hard working
Reliable & self-motivated
Team player
Have a mature & responsible attitude
Good communicator
Able to follow instructions
Car and Licence
Have a tolerance to working in a poultry environment, which can be dusty, noisy and smelly
good understanding of English, both written and oral
About Darwalla
Darwalla has a diverse workforce and welcomes applicants who are as passionate as we are about producing quality chickens. As a fourth-generation family business, Darwalla started producing chickens over 90 years in Mt Cotton, with farms now located in the Redlands, on the Darling Downs and in the Lockyer Valley.
https://darwalla.expr3ss.com/jobDetails?selectJob=95&s=6

title: Queensland (QLD)
description:
12 fév.
Farm Attendant
The role:
Join Tassal’s Mission Beach Prawn Farm as a Farm Attendant, where you’ll play a hands-on role in ensuring optimal prawn health and farm operations. This position is perfect for team-oriented individuals who thrive in outdoor, physically active environments and are passionate about sustainable aquaculture. You’ll work as part of a safety-focused team to maintain our premium prawn production, with tasks ranging from feeding and health checks to farm maintenance and record-keeping.
This is a casual role which will involve working flexible shifts which could include days/afternoons, nights & weekends.
Responsibilities:
· Monitor and feed prawns, conduct health checks, and report any concerns.
· Perform general farm maintenance and upkeep of ponds and equipment.
· Accurately log farm data using iPads to track prawn growth and conditions.
· Work safely and efficiently as part of a close-knit team to meet production goals.
· Uphold Tassal’s Zero Harm culture in all daily activities.
Role requirements:
· Comfortable working in hot, humid, and wet conditions (including wading in ponds).
· Physical fitness for manual labour and outdoor work.
· Attention to detail for monitoring prawn health and maintaining records.
· Driver’s licence (manual preferred) and reliable transport to site.
· Flexibility to work rotating shifts (days, nights, weekends).
· Team player with a safety-first mindset.
· The legal right to work in Australia.
Desirable
· Forklift ticket and experience.
· Prior experience in aquaculture, farming, or outdoor labour.
Role offerings:
· Join a welcoming, inclusive crew where collaboration and innovation are celebrated—your contributions matter here.
· Gain hands-on experience with training, upskilling, and clear pathways for professional growth.
· Access our holistic health programs, including mental health support, fitness initiatives, and family wellbeing resources.
· Enjoy the stability of working with Australia’s premier sustainable aquaculture company, where your development is invested in.
· Contribute to meaningful work that prioritises environmental stewardship and ethical food production.
Company culture:
Our people each contribute in different ways, embracing our business values and harnessing our can do – safely culture with purpose and intent. Our shared values are an essential part of our business culture, they define us. They underpin and guide our commitment, attitude, how we work and the quality of our products. We are passionate, we achieve together, we own it and we can do it safely.
We encourage early applications as we review on a daily basis.
If you have any questions or wish to apply, please email Jeff at Talent@tassal.com.au
At Tassal Group, we are dedicated to “Sustainably Feeding Tomorrow.”
With over 35 years of aquaculture experience, we focus on providing healthy, sustainable, and nutritious food, including salmon, prawn, and barramundi. We are committed to producing accessible food, supporting communities, and delivering strong economic results, all while prioritising sustainability to ensure a prosperous, healthy planet for future generations.

title: Queensland (QLD)
description:
10 fév.
Farm Hand
We’re seeking energetic and reliable casual Farm Hands to join our macadamia orchard team near Gogango, Queensland. This is a great opportunity to earn money while gaining hands-on farming experience in a friendly, supportive, and safety-focused environment.
Whether you’re new to agriculture or looking to build practical skills in horticulture, this role offers valuable experience in a growing industry.
Key Responsibilities
Reporting to the Orchard Manager, you will be involved in:
Assisting with the growth and care of macadamia trees
Learning to safely operate farm machinery (training provided – no prior experience required)
Supporting pruning activities and general farm maintenance
Working collaboratively as part of a safety-first team
About You
To be successful in this role, you will have:
Clear spoken English with good comprehension
A valid C-class driver’s licence
A strong work ethic and willingness to learn
Good attention to detail and a hands-on attitude
Flexibility to work early starts and occasional Saturdays
Previous farm experience is advantageous but not essential
What We Offer
Casual award rates and overtime in accordance with the Horticulture Award
FREE daily transport from Rockhampton, Gracemere, Stanwell, and Westwood
Full on-the-job training and ongoing support
A welcoming team and positive work environment
An opportunity to gain practical experience you’ll genuinely value
How to Apply
Please submit your resume and a cover letter addressing the key responsibilities and requirements of the role.
Please note: Applications will be reviewed as they are received.
P: (02) 6203 9700
E: people@ruralfunds.com.au
About Rural Funds Management (RFM)
Rural Funds Management (RFM) is an industry leader managing approximately $2.6 billion in agricultural assets on behalf of retail and institutional investors, including the ASX-listed Rural Funds Group (RFF).
With over 28 years’ experience, RFM owns, develops, and operates farms across most Australian states.
Our culture is built on the principle of “Managing good assets with good people.” We value precision, variety, effort, and teamwork, and we’re proud to provide a friendly and inclusive workplace.
Joining RFM means becoming part of a team of more than 250 professionals, including fund managers, finance specialists, horticulturists, agronomists, and farm managers — all working together to deliver sustainable agricultural success.

title: Queensland (QLD)
description:
9 fév.
🎯 Chargé(e) de Marketing Créatif – 20h – 25h /semaine | Poste basé à Brisbane
📅 Démarrage : Mars 2026
Mission : minimum 6 mois
Rémunération : Award rate Casual
Tu as déjà de l’expérience en création de contenu et marketing digital ?
Tu sais gérer des projets créatifs dans un cadre structuré, produire du contenu de qualité et piloter plusieurs marques à la fois ?
Tu es basé(e) à Brisbane et tu veux t’impliquer dans une équipe pro, dynamique, et tournée vers l’humain ? Cette mission est faite pour toi.
Qui sommes-nous ?
Nous accompagnons les jeunes Français qui viennent vivre en Australie, à travers trois marques complémentaires :
My Little France Australie (services d’installation WHV)
Stradbroke Island Tours (tours nature et culturels)
Backpacars (achat/revente de véhicules backpackers)
Notre communication est très active (Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, YouTube, newsletters, blog…) et nous cherchons un(e) professionnel(le) pour renforcer la production de contenu tout en garantissant cohérence, qualité et impact.
🚀 Ta mission
Sous la responsabilité de notre Responsable Marketing, tu prendras en charge la création de contenu pour les 3 marques :
Décliner une stratégie de contenu cohérente et différenciante sur chaque canal
Créer des visuels et vidéos (stories, reels, shorts…) adaptés à nos personas
Réaliser du montage vidéo (CapCut, InShot, etc.)
Être à l’aise face caméra pour certaines séquences ou capsules informatives
Rédiger du contenu écrit : newsletters, articles de blog, publications sociales
Participer à l’amélioration continue de la ligne éditoriale, du ton et des formats
Assurer la veille concurrentielle et créative pour rester à la page côté backpacker, tourisme & digital
💡 Ton profil
Expérience de minimum 1 à 2 ans dans un poste en marketing digital, contenu ou communication visuelle
Très à l’aise avec les outils Canva, CapCut, InShot, Notion, Google Drive, Meta Business Suite, Many Chat etc
Tu as déjà géré des comptes de marques, ou contribué à des campagnes à fort engagement
Tu sais travailler avec une équipe, prendre des briefs, livrer dans les temps
Tu es autonome, structuré.e, organisé.e et créatif.ve
Tu maîtrises parfaitement le français écrit et oral et bon niveau en anglais
Tu es basé(e) à Brisbane avec un visa de travail valide en Australie (WHV, student, partner…)
✉️ Pour postuler
Merci d’envoyer à info@mylittlefrance.com.au :
Ton CV
Des références de projets ou contenus créés : comptes, campagnes, vidéos, newsletters, etc.
Un mot de motivation (court) : pourquoi ce poste te parle et comment tu t’imagines contribuer aux 3 marques

title: New South Wales (NSW)
description:
6 j
Cleaner - Guyra NSW
Position description Apply now
We are seeking enthusiastic and dedicated cleaner's to join our team on a regular and on call basis.
Duties:
Cleaning duties will be focused on amenities.
Includes cleaning and sanitising meal rooms after each use, toilets, wash areas, floors, high traffic areas, disposing of rubbish, refilling consumables etc.
The ideal candidate will possess:
Availability on short notice
No allergies to cleaning chemicals and PPE
Have own reliable transport to and from work
Strong commitment to WHS and Hygiene standards and procedures
About Costa
We’re Costa. Australia’s leading grower, packer and marketer of premium quality fresh fruit and vegetables.
On the land, in distribution centres, and out on the roads – Costa people work hard to bring the freshest produce to tables across Australia.
But joining our team is about more than just hard work. It’s about picking a meaningful, sustainable job that’s good for you, and for Australia.
Costa is committed to sustainable agriculture. Not only is it a key strategy for maximising our profitability, it’s also crucial to ensuring that our country’s natural resources are utilised in both an environmentally responsible and efficient manner. By taking this approach, Costa grows products which are healthy, safe and of the highest quality, while at the same time protecting and improving the natural environment.
With over 60 farms across Australia, our produce categories include:
Berries (Blueberries, Raspberries, Blackberries, Strawberries)
Mushrooms
Tomatoes
Avocados
Bananas
Grapes
Citrus (Oranges, Lemons, Mandarins, Tangelos, Limes, Grapefruit)
Our produce is supplied to all the major Australian supermarket chains, as well as independent grocers and a range of food industry stakeholders. We also export to Asia, North America and Europe.
About the Tomato Category
Costa supplies tomatoes 365 days a year under the Blush brand and private label brands, including our Perino range and Cocktail truss in Coles, Sweet Berry truss and Cocktail truss in Woolworths and our Bellino range and Cocktail truss in ALDI.
Our tomatoes are grown in one of Australia’s largest and most advanced glasshouse facilities, located in the New South Wales town of Guyra.  A $67 million expansion project commenced during 2019 which will increase our production area by 33% and create an additional 150 jobs onsite.
Our products are also grown in South Australia, Tasmania, Queensland Western Australia through our grower partners.
Our state-of-the-art glasshouse technology puts Costa Tomatoes at the forefront of innovation in ecologically sustainable practices and sets us apart in the fruit and vegetable industry.
For further information or a confidential discussion, please contact
Pete Davies, Glass house Services Manager - Vertical Farming
PH: 0499 940 426
E: Peter.Davis@costagroup.com.au

title: New South Wales (NSW)
description:
25 mars
NURSERY HAND - multiple roles
We're looking for motivated nursery hands who have an genuine interest in plants or horticulture to join our team.
Who we are
Scotts Tubes is a leading wholesale production nursery based at Mangrove Mountain, NSW.
We grow over 1,000 lines of speciality flowering plants, herbs, vegetables, perennial and upstarts tubestock.
Our plants are supplied to retail chain stores and independent retailers across NSW and the ACT.
What you'll be doing
We are looking to fill multiple roles across different teams, the type of work you'll be doing can vary, but can include tasks like:
picking, sorting, packing and dispatching of customer plant orders
all aspects of plant production and care: seeding, planting, watering etc
maintenance and cleaning of dispatch and production areas
mule and/or tractor driving
operating planting machinery and using hand-tools
general nursery duties as needed.
Days and hours
Work hours are 6:30am start to 3:00pm - breaks at designated times.
These positions are 5 days per week, Monday to Friday.
Positions will start as casual but can become ongoing for the right person.
In our busy seasons, overtime hours are usually available.
What we're looking for
Nursery hands that:
have a genuine interest in the horticulture industry
have nursery or gardening experience
are reliable and have their own transport (public transport is not an option to our location)
have the physical ability to undertake manual tasks, like lifting and moving heavy items, walking and being on your feet all day, in all weather conditions.
have an eye for detail
can learn quickly, take instructions and follow a processes
work well in a fast-paced environment
can work independently, as well as in a team
display good communication skills
have Australian work rights.
Desirable (not essential)
Certificate 3 in Horticulture (or equivalent).
Industry experience.
Knowledge of plant varieties - herb knowledge an advantage.
How to apply
To be considered, your application must include:
a cover letter introducing yourself and why you're applying for a job with us, and
an up-to-date resume.
Email the above to: greg@scottstubes.com.au.

Fulltime Dairy Farm Hand
Mount View Dairies – Full Time Dairy Farm Employee
Mount View Dairies is seeking a reliable and motivated person to join our team. Our farm is literally a stone’s throw from the well-serviced town of Gloucester on the beautiful Mid North Coast of NSW.
We are aiming to grow to a 150 cow herd this season. We operate a pasture-based system with supplementary feeding in the dairy and on the feed pad. We take pride in running a tidy, well-organised dairy with excellent animal welfare, great milk quality and a calm working environment. We love our cows and focus heavily on their health, well being and genetics and we are looking for someone who will love them as much as we do.
This is an all-round role suited to someone who enjoys working with cows and takes pride in doing things properly. You will be working as part of a small, supportive farm team.
About the farm
• Located right on the edge of Gloucester on the NSW Mid North Coast
• Growing to a 150 cow herd this season
• Pasture-based system with feed pad and mixer wagon
• Focus on excellent cow health and milk quality
• Small, supportive team
• Well maintained facilities and equipment
Key tasks and responsibilities
• Milking cows
• Monitoring cow health and drafting cows when required
• Calf rearing
• Feeding silage using a mixer wagon
• Strip grazing
• Irrigation
• Fencing, water and general farm maintenance
• General tractor paddock work such as slashing and mulching
What we are looking for
• Reliable with a strong work ethic
• Good stock sense and calm animal handling
• Able to work independently and follow farm systems
• Attention to detail
• Positive attitude and willingness to learn
Previous dairy experience is preferred but not essential.
Traineeships will also be considered for the right candidate.
This is a long-term permanent position and is not suitable for backpackers at this time.
Conditions
• Wages paid under the Pastoral Award depending on experience and qualifications
• Accommodation may be available for the right applicant
• 10 day fortnight roster with every second weekend off
• Mixture of morning and/or afternoon milkings
Start date: April 2026
The role would suit someone who enjoys working with animals, takes pride in doing things properly, and wants to be part of a well-run farm.
If you think this could be you, please send your resume and a short expression of interest to:
adam.forbes1991@gmail.com

title: New South Wales (NSW)
description:
13 mars
Irrigation Hand - Hillston, NSW
The Role
We are seeking a skilled and reliable Irrigation Hand to join our client's almond farm in Hillston, NSW. Working outdoors as part of the irrigation team, you'll play a key role in installing, maintaining, and repairing irrigation systems to keep crops thriving. This is a practical, hands-on role suited to someone who enjoys physical work, problem-solving, and contributing to a supportive farm team environment.
Key Responsibilities
In this role, you will help ensure efficient water management and smooth irrigation operations across the farm, including:
Assisting with installation and maintenance of irrigation systems (sprinklers, piping, valves, timers)
Conducting headland checks and driving a mule for field inspections
Performing routine maintenance and troubleshooting issues with irrigation equipment
Mixing and applying fertilisers through irrigation systems
Supporting repairs and adjustments to maintain optimal water coverage and efficiency
Maintaining accurate records and following Quality, Health, Safety and Environment systems
Benefits
$30.35 per hour plus penalty rates
Approx. 3 months of work with consistent hours
30-40 hours per week, with potential for up to 6 days per week
Work outdoors in a team-focused farm environment
Chance to develop further skills in irrigation and agricultural systems
Your Skills & Experience
We are looking for candidates who are reliable, safety-conscious, and able to adapt to varied farm tasks. Desired but not essential skills include:
Qualifications and/or experience in irrigation or open space management
Ability to read farm maps and follow work plans
Clear commitment to workplace safety and quality systems
Knowledge of irrigation systems (sprinkler, drip, etc.) and water management principles
Experience operating relevant tools or machinery (preferred but not essential)
Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail

description:

Cotton Warehouse Loader Operator - Whitton, NSW
The Role
We are seeking an experienced Loader Operator to join a busy cotton warehouse operation in Whitton, NSW. This is a hands-on role working in a fast-paced warehouse environment where you'll operate loaders and machinery to move cotton and seed while assisting with general site operations. The team works around the clock during peak season, so flexibility and reliability are essential.
Key Responsibilities
This role supports the safe and efficient movement of cotton and materials within the warehouse while assisting with general operational tasks.
Operate loader machinery to move cotton bales and seed
Monitor equipment using gauges and operational systems
Conduct daily pre-start checks and basic machine inspections
Ensure goods are stored correctly according to warehouse requirements
Maintain site cleanliness and safe working conditions
The ability to clearly communicate operational updates to the Site Manager and team via two way radio
Moon buggy relief driving
Benefits
Hourly rate of $33.91 + Shift loading
Approximately 12 weeks
Rotating shift roster with approximately 38-60 hours per week depending on operational needs
Work within a structured cotton warehouse environment
On-site accommodation option is available
This is a casual position paid under the Cotton Ginning Award. The casual hourly rate will apply during the off-season, with the applicable seasonal rate applying once the ginning season begins, in accordance with the Award.
Your Skills & Experience
To succeed in this role, you will be physically capable, reliable, and confident operating machinery in a warehouse or agricultural environment.
Proven experience operating loader or heavy machinery
Forklift licence preferred
Ability to work in physically demanding environments, including heat and outdoor conditions
Willingness to work flexible hours, weekends, and overtime during peak periods
Strong focus on workplace safety and equipment checks
Reliable, punctual, and able to work effectively in a team
Ability to pass pre-employment medical testing including drug & alcohol screening
Own reliable transport to travel to the worksite

title: New South Wales (NSW)
description:
11 mars
Labourer, Rural Contracting
Copper Creek Contracting provides farm infrastructure services to high performing farmers, landowners and businesses in Central West NSW. We specialise in fencing, stockyards construction and rural infrastructure projects with the aim of ensuring every client is a return and referring client.
The position
Continued growth and increased work load has opened a position for a Labourer (casual) within our team. Our Labourer (casual) will:
Assist in the completion of fencing, stockyards construction and rural infrastructure projects to specifications and appropriate industry standards.
Assist in the preparation, use and maintenance of vehicles, equipment and tooling.
Undertake general labouring duties as required.
Work side by side with our trade qualified Team Leaders to deliver projects
This is a casual position with full time hours offered and the potential for ongoing and permanent work for the right candidate.
With a number of upcoming projects throughout the Central West and neighbouring regions, you must be willing and able to travel for work. (All travel, accommodation and meals are provided when travelling for work).
Our culture
At the core of our team are mutual respect, continuous improvement and shared values. We work together with a can-do attitude to deliver our best each day. We are flexible in our approach and adaptable to the needs of our team-mates and clients. We are each valued, supported and encouraged to become our best selves.
We set ourselves up to have a great day, every day.
We offer above award wages, flexible working conditions, training and a supportive environment with future growth opportunities.
What our team say about working with us:
"I have genuinely appreciated the opportunities for growth and the support provided to me during my time here"
"Working with such a dedicated team has been both professionally and personally rewarding"
To learn more about our team and what we do check out our Facebook and Instagram accounts: Copper Creek Contracting
About you
With experience or an interest in agriculture, construction or metal fabrication (or all!) you will be looking for your next challenge or opportunity. You will thrive on variety and have a can-do attitude. You will be aiming to develop your skills and knowledge across a variety of projects, tasks and skillsets and will work proactively and positively with our team and clients.
You will be well presented with sober habits and be looking for a long term career in the agriculture or construction industries.
Selection criteria
Essential:
Ability to work as an effective team member.
Ability to prioritise work and complete tasks in a timely manner.
Effective communication and interpersonal skills.
Practical skills using hand and power tools.
Fit to work outside and undertake manual tasks.
Willingness to learn and take direction. Ability to learn new skills and undertake formal and informal training.
Clean and sober habits.
Current and valid NSW Drivers Licence (Manual P1 Licence as a minimum) and own reliable transport.
Willing and able to travel for work.
Highly desirable:
Experience and skills, or interest, in any of the following areas:
Fencing
Concreting
Machinery and vehicle operation
Roofing
Carpentry or construction
Previous experience in an agricultural, construction or metal fabrication environment highly regarded.
Immediate start available for the right candidate.
To discuss this position please contact Jess on 0403 496 549.
This position is NOT suitable for individuals not currently located in Australia. Please note, we do not have accommodation for local work.
Employer questions
Your application will include the following questions:
How many years' experience do you have as a Labourer?
Which of the following statements best describes your right to work in Australia?
Do you own or have regular access to a car?
Are you willing to undergo pre-employment drug and alcohol screening?
Do you hold a Construction Induction Safety certificate (White Card)?
Are you available to travel for this role when required?
Are you willing to undergo a pre-employment medical check?
Do you have a current Australian driver's licence?

Farm Hand Machinery Operator
2hrs from Perth near the town of Williams WA, our farming business is now looking for a farm hand machinery operator. We have 3200ha cropping and 7,000 sheep. We have modern and safe equipment including an enclosed drive through workshop with clean comfortable lunch areas.
This job comes with on farm accommodation. There is a new kitchen, and the rooms are freshly painted and have air conditioning. We supply food free of charge. It is a historical building which has individual rooms with a shared bathroom.
We are looking for someone skilled and experienced as a farm hand operating tractors.
Main requirements,
Be procedural, we use written procedure guides for most operations.
Operate broadacre machinery at a good standard.
Mechanical skills in one of these areas– Greasing and checking fluid levels, using tools to change bolts, steel fabrication.
Some livestock skills – handling sheep in the yards.
Good communication and listening skills
Can manage long hours of operating machinery alone, competently and safely.
The machines to operate in this role are,
JCB Fastrac with Marshal multi spreader
JCB Fastrac with Goldacre sprayer.
There are general farm duties with this role that are
· Operating telehandlers for grain hay and dirt
· Greasing and maintaining any machinery.
· Repairing roads.
· Cleaning around sheds and work sites
· Fencing, including post drilling and concreting
· Repairing sheds and buildings.
Hours - Monday to Friday 7.30am to 4.30pm when there is not much to do. Busy seeding time like March April May is 12 hours per day 6 days per week with Sundays off. This role will need some afternoon night shifts to be done (midday to midnight).
The job usually runs for 6 to 12 months depending on farm requirements.
We are a family owned and managed business. We incorporate flexible work hours to share the load and use casual workers for help with all operations. Owner, manager has a Christian faith.
It will be great to have you on board with us to enjoy our farming lifestyle. Please apply to willfowler1@hotmail.com with your resume or cover letter for further information. I will get back to you as soon as possible. Thankyou

title: Western Australia (WA)
description:
11 mars
Casual Stablehand - Weekends
Company Description
About Us
The racing world is not just exciting, it brings communities together across the state. Racing and Wagering Western Australia (RWWA) is at the centre of this world and more than 24,000 people choose to join us in our quest to forge a sustainable future for racing in WA.
Our business is unique, which means we can offer careers which are interesting, challenging and diverse. Our values are important to us so, to be successful, you’ll have to share them. We respect one another, are accountable for our goals, connecting with everyone and bringing excellence to achieve great outcomes. We work hard but have fun while we’re doing it.
Job Description
About the role
OTTWA is looking for an experienced and positive person to join our small, committed team in North Dandalup for Casual weekend work as a Stablehand .
To achieve success in the role you will need to demonstrate your experience in:
Undertaking day-to-day duties for care of horses including checking, feeding and rugging at weekends.
Your attention to detail in maintaining high levels of horse care.
Ensuring the general wellbeing of horses is high, including identifying where horses may be injured or require medical attention and escalating such concerns as and when they arise as directed by your manager
Assisting in all aspects of Off the track estate programs where required
Qualifications
About You
Minimum two years experience working with horses in a similar role (preference with thoroughbred or standardbred horses)
Experience in assessing a horse’s level of handling and temperament.
Knowledge of general horse care and husbandry
Ability to work individually or unsupervised and manage time autonomously and as a proactive team member
Self-motivation and initiative
Demonstrated ability to prioritise tasks.
Prepared to work outside of normal business days/hours, including at short notice if required.
Current ‘C’ class driver’s license
Be physically fit and able to cope with the physical demands of the role
Attention to detail and a high standard of care
Proficiency with farm machinery and an understanding of maintenance standards required for a horse property - preferred.
An understanding of WA thoroughbred or harness racing industry- beneficial.
Additional Information
What are you waiting for?
Apply now with your CV and cover letter telling us why you’re the perfect fit for this opportunity.
For more information visit Careers at RWWA or contact us at TalentAcquisition@rwwa.com.au
As part of the recruitment process, you will be required to complete Pre-Employment screening, which will include two reference checks, police clearance and Australian Working Rights check.
Please note this role is only open to applicants that hold Australian Working Rights and we are unable to offer Work Sponsorship Visa's.
Additional benefits
Health and wellness initiatives to support you inside and out
Reward and Recognition Program
At Racing and Wagering WA, we recognise that we are strengthened by diversity of gender, thought, experience, and ethnicity. We are committed to providing a safe work environment and making reasonable adjustments in which everyone is included and treated fairly.
We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and encourage women, people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent and people from diverse backgrounds to apply.
We are a 2025 Circle Back Initiative Employer - we commit to respond to every applicant.
Applications Open until filled

title: Western Australia (WA)
description:
6 mars
Seeding / Tractor Operator WA Central Wheatbelt – Broadacre Cropping
Seeding / Tractor Operator WA Central Wheatbelt – Broadacre Cropping Enterprise
Join a large-scale Western Australian cropping enterprise operating modern New Holland articulated tractors, 40–70ft seeding bars and GPS precision farming technology across a large scale cropping program in the Central Wheatbelt, approximately three hours north-east of Perth.
Staff 360 are currently seeking an experienced Seeding / Tractor Operators to join this progressive farming business for the current seeding program.
The farm utilises modern machinery and technology-driven farming systems, along with a large sheep enterprise running alongside the cropping program.
This role will suit an experienced machinery operator who enjoys working with large modern equipment, technology and broadacre cropping systems.
Key Responsibilities
• Operating modern articulated tractors with seeding bars and large air seeder systems
• Assisting with broadacre seeding operations across a large-scale cropping program
• Operating farm trucks and assisting with logistics during seeding
• Preparing and maintaining seeding equipment before and during the season
• Conducting daily machinery checks and assisting with servicing
• Working with GPS guidance and precision farming technology
• Supporting general farm operations when required
Skills & Experience
• Previous experience operating broadacre seeding equipment
• Experience operating large articulated tractors and air seeders
• Familiarity with GPS-guided machinery and modern farm technology
• HC licence highly regarded
• Mechanical aptitude and ability to assist with machinery servicing
• Ability to work long hours during peak farming periods
• Positive attitude and ability to work within a team environment
The Opportunity
• Competitive remuneration based on experience
• Accommodation provided
• Access to farm vehicles where required
• Work with modern John Deer, New Holland machinery and large-scale equipment
• Join a progressive broadacre cropping and livestock operation
How to Apply To apply for this position please email applications to: admin@staff360.com.au and attach your current resume with work related references.
For further information please contact Staff 360 on 07 3720 8504
Staff 360 specialise in providing quality personnel to the global agricultural industry. Over the past 25 years we have supplied skilled and experienced staff to a range of positions within the agricultural sector. Based in Brisbane we have clients and job opportunities spread throughout Australia and internationally within livestock, farming and horticulture sectors.

title: Western Australia (WA)
description:
24 fév.
Tree Planter
About the Company
Timberwolf is Australia's largest nationally recognised tree-planting company, delivering large-scale reforestation and environmental restoration projects across QLD, NSW, VIC, SA, and WA. We get down and dirty to make a real difference.
We plant in a wide range of environments including wetlands, streetscapes, parks, and regeneration sites, working with government, councils, and private clients to create greener, more resilient landscapes.
Benefits
Flexible roster supporting work–life balance
Piece-rate pay structure - earn more based on productivity
Guaranteed hourly award rate of $31.19
Opportunities to travel Australia for regional and away work
Work outdoors in a physically active role
Join a supportive, team-focused work environment
About the Role
Tree planting is not for the faint-hearted. As a Tree Planter, you will work outdoors in varying terrain and weather conditions, planting trees as part of a production-based team. This is a physically demanding role suited to individuals who enjoy hands-on work and are motivated by performance-based earnings.
No two days are the same, and you'll work alongside people from diverse backgrounds on meaningful environmental projects.
Key Responsibilities
Manual planting of trees across varied sites
Meeting daily production targets while maintaining quality standards
Working safely and efficiently as part of a team
Following site plans, safety procedures, and supervisor instructions
Skills & Experience
No prior experience required as full training is provided just come motivated, tough, and maybe a little wild.
Physically fit and capable of sustained manual labour
Strong work ethic and reliability
Ability to work outdoors in all weather conditions
Willingness to travel for work (desirable)
Requirements
Applicants must have or be willing to obtain:
Construction White Card.
Valid driver's licence and a reliable vehicle.
Full PPE (or willingness to purchase).
Applicants must have Australian work rights.
How to Apply
Visit https://www.timberwolfplanting.com.au/join-the-pack to apply for more information.
Join Timberwolf and help build a greener Australia.

Casual Kitchen Hand / Cleaner (Commercial Bakery) North Fremantle
Blossom Fine Foods – North Fremantle
Hours: Casual – 4–5 days per week (Mon–Thu / Fri), 9:30am–4:00pm
Pay: $31.19 per hour + Super
Location: North Fremantle (easy access via public transport routes)
Blossom Fine Foods is looking for a reliable Kitchen Hand / Cleaner to help keep our bakery/kitchen spotless, organised, and running smoothly. If you’re a true clean freak with sharp attention to detail and you genuinely enjoy cleaning and keeping things tidy, we’d love to hear from you.
About the role
This is a hands-on cleaning and kitchen support role in a busy commercial food environment. You’ll help maintain high hygiene standards and assist with essential back-of-house tasks.
Key duties
Keeping the commercial kitchen clean, hygienic and presentable throughout the day
Supporting the dishwasher during busy periods (washing, stacking, putting away)
Scrubbing, washing and maintaining tins/trays (including baked-on residue)
De-moulding products and traying up tins as required
Cleaning benches, floors, sinks, walls and high-touch areas
Following food safety, hygiene and cleaning schedules
Using commercial cleaning products/chemicals safely and correctly
General tidy-up duties to keep everything in its place
What we’re looking for
A strong work ethic and high standards of cleanliness (you notice the little things)
Commercial kitchen cleaning experience preferred
Confident using cleaning chemicals and following safety procedures
Physically fit and strong — some heavy lifting involved (trays/tins)
Reliable, punctual, and works well as part of a team
Able to keep pace in a busy kitchen environment
Must be available for all rostered shifts: Mon–Thu / Fri, 9:30am–4:00pm (4–5 days per week)
To apply
Please email only to angie@blossomfinefoods.com.au with:
A short cover letter outlining your experience
Confirmation you can work all required shifts listed above
Your resume (if you have one)
